name: recommended
doc_loadout: only the rules needed for the dominant failure
prompt:
You are resuming this writing project through the `repair-cycle` helper packet.
Live docs outrank sidecar and checkpoint evidence whenever they disagree.
Do not mutate canon or current-doc files, and do not perform silent writes.
Use this exact command first: `writing-sidecar routine "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --name repair-cycle --verify advisory`
If you later want the sidecar-safe persisted version, use: `writing-sidecar routine "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --name repair-cycle --verify advisory --write`
Use this packet to identify the dominant failure, set the repair direction, and keep rejected material cut unless evidence says otherwise.
Open these files first:
- <PROJECT_ROOT>\_story_bible\05_Current_Notes.md
- <PROJECT_ROOT>\_story_bible\05_Current_Chapter_Notes.md
Next phase-local command after this packet: `writing-sidecar session "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --task debug`
Use the exact commands supplied by this helper packet instead of inventing new workflow steps.
expected_outputs: An audit/debug helper packet that names the dominant failure and likely repair direction.
The exact audit-safe artifact target when you choose to persist the repair loop.
The debug-facing follow-up commands without silently writing cut-path artifacts.
recommended_actions: Use the audit-loop bundle to identify the dominant failure, then use the debug packet to scope the repair instead of broad polish.
Write the audit artifact only after the dominant failure is explicit.
Apply the explicit fact ops through a write-capable command when the preview looks right.
Open the suggested loadout before continuing.
Move to writing-sidecar session "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --task debug --write when the dominant failure is clear.
Run writing-sidecar session "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --task debug --write to preserve the repair pass.
Add --note "..." if you reject a concrete structure and want it preserved in discarded_paths.
Return to writing-sidecar session "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --task audit after the dominant failure is repaired.
